112576.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With as many men as women. Such a government represents all perspectives to make fair decisions.

112577.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

112580.explanation: With an overloaded power grid, it is primarily the municipality's task to encourage residents and businesses to use less energy. Progress can be made here. We help households that are unable to do so.

112581.explanation: There is temporary shelter and after that it will move to another location for permanent shelter. This way we more than meet our obligations and consider our municipality's capacity. So no extra asylum seeker center will be built.

112592.explanation: We want music lessons to be accessible not only to children with wealthy parents. But giving every child music lessons would overshoot our goal. An expansion of our offer at schools and outside school hours is more appropriate.

112593.explanation: Associations are the cornerstone of our community spirit. We do not write a blank check, but we help where we can and what is reasonable so they can continue to fulfill their important role.

112596.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. Cameras can contribute to that. We prefer more street enforcement officers who can immediately intervene when something goes wrong. But we do not rule out extra cameras if they help.

112602.explanation: Converting offices into housing certainly offers possibilities to add extra homes to the housing market. However, no subsidy from the municipality is needed for that. Fast procedures and clear rules are necessary.

112603.explanation: We want to help people, where possible, from welfare to work. This benefits them financially and socially. However, obliging everyone to socially meaningful work is too black-and-white and does not do justice to the problems some welfare recipients have.

112604.explanation: With this scheme, we specifically help people who cannot make ends meet. However, we want to look at an intermediate rate. Currently, you pay 100% tax or 0%. By introducing something in between, the gap (poverty trap) is smaller and working pays off sooner.

112610.explanation: An environmental zone is not the solution here. We must start by streamlining the supply to the center. For example, with a (green) inner-city distribution. This reduces much traffic in and through the center, making it safer and cleaner.

112611.explanation: When it comes to healthcare, you must always maintain freedom of choice. The quality of care is leading. So within the contracted offerings, attention must also be given to this.

112612.explanation: We must look at effectiveness. Cheap is often expensive in the long run. The effect of help is sometimes only known after 1 or 2 years. Because only if no new help requests arise after that assistance can you say whether something has been effective. So if we measure well, we can also select better providers.

112613.explanation: We are already doing this through 'Menstruation Distribution Points' (MUPs). This works well and we will continue this.

112617.explanation: Companies have difficulty expanding and new companies cannot establish themselves. However, the new location is very important. We are critical of the proposed location near Gaanderen.

112618.explanation: This is currently still standing (national) policy. However, we need to look at a fair distribution key per municipality. It should not be the case that young people have to live at home for years at the expense of this group.

112619.explanation: Litter on the street is annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. It is much better for people to bring their waste so the municipality can properly separate and process it. This results in clean streets and a healthy environment. Therefore, we believe the recycling center should remain free for private individuals.

112620.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

112624.explanation: We currently only have 2 coffeeshops. When one closes (temporarily), the other has a monopoly and street dealing worsens. That is why we need 3 shops.

112629.explanation: The parking garages (Amphion and Catharina) are often still empty. So more parking spaces are not needed.

112630.explanation: We need to start streamlining the provisioning of the city center. For example, with (green) inner-city distribution. This reduces much traffic in and through the center, making it safer and cleaner.

112631.explanation: We certainly think this is worth investigating. In other parts of the country, this has proven to have positive effects.

112632.explanation: This prevents young people from leaving the city. With a strongly aging region, youth now more than ever have the future. Priority for teachers, doctors, and other crucial professions is also seen as promising.

112633.explanation: We are in favor of wind energy but are critical of this location. We are not yet convinced that sufficient account is taken of the (possible) health risks for residents and the impact on nature. We want to redo the research on search areas.

112634.explanation: A ring road on the west side could greatly reduce traffic congestion elsewhere. Then it is also easier to reduce other roads to 30 km/h.

112576.explanation: For the CDA, it is about the best possible governance for Doetinchem. A reflection of society is nice but not mandatory.

112577.explanation: The CDA has always been against referenda but in favor of participation via, for example, participation processes and hearing residents' opinions through consultation and online platforms.

112580.explanation: The CDA supports facilitating residents in such processes. This does not necessarily have to be financial support.

112581.explanation: As long as the dispersion law is in effect, we adhere to it. This does not mean that an asylum seeker center must necessarily be established. Doetinchem has often taken its responsibility in the past.

112592.explanation: The music school already receives substantial subsidies, which keeps music lesson prices lower. For the CDA, this is sufficient because we must use residents' money prudently and distribute it as fairly as possible.

112593.explanation: Sports clubs are of great importance to the CDA. They are not only for sports but also for social cohesion and meeting others. Sports clubs should be able to receive help, for example, replacing their fields.

112596.explanation: The CDA has always supported camera surveillance in locations where it can increase safety. In our opinion, there are still places where camera surveillance can be expanded.

112602.explanation: In Doetinchem, partly due to the CDA, several offices have already been converted into homes. If there is an unprofitable surplus (it really costs too much), we believe the government should be able to help make the conversion possible.

112603.explanation: The CDA is a strong supporter of the counter-performance. This way, you give people a purpose while simultaneously contributing something to our society. This can definitely be volunteer work.

112604.explanation: The CDA finds it unrealistic to stop full remission altogether. However, we must continue to see if it remains bearable for the other residents who do not receive remission. The shortfalls caused by the remission are borne by the other residents.

112610.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of additional regulations concerning environmental zones.

112611.explanation: More than half of our budget now goes to the social domain. The CDA believes we should provide qualitatively good care at an affordable price. That is our primary principle. Extra rules on top increase costs.

112612.explanation: The quality of youth care is safeguarded in legislation. The municipality must maintain this quality and primarily purchase that high-quality care as affordably as possible so it remains accessible to all residents.

112613.explanation: There are great social initiatives for people with limited means, such as the mini manna market and the food bank. The CDA sees no role for the government in providing free tampons and sanitary pads.

112617.explanation: Companies need space. In our view, this can be done through the expansion of existing sites and their revitalization. If new areas are needed, this should be addressed regionally.

112618.explanation: The CDA finds it increasingly difficult to justify that youth must wait long on waiting lists while refugees are given priority. The CDA would like to handle this more carefully and create more balance if allowed by the national government.

112619.explanation: According to the CDA, nothing is really free. Someone has to pay the bill. However, we want costs to be as low as possible and must closely monitor affordability.

112620.explanation: The CDA has never been a strong supporter of solar parks on land. As far as we're concerned, we have reached the maximum number of parks now and no new parks will be added.

112624.explanation: Recently, there has been unrest around the coffeeshops. The CDA has always opposed the establishment of additional coffeeshops.

112629.explanation: Now that more homes are being built around the center, these residents must also be able to park their cars properly. The CDA therefore wants more parking spaces around the center.

112630.explanation: The real city center is already car-free at this moment. However, this downtown must remain accessible and plays a major role in the traffic flow through Doetinchem. The CDA is therefore against a completely car-free center.

112631.explanation: Doetinchem has many facilities for homeless people. We believe that any anti-social behavior housing is also a task for municipalities in our region since homelessness is a regional responsibility; they have space and Doetinchem already has many facilities and currently provides almost all shelter.

112632.explanation: The housing market is under enormous pressure. We see waiting times increasing. The CDA favors granting priority to local residents where possible in allocation. This should be discussed with Sité in the performance agreements.

112633.explanation: The CDA has always held the position that we first await new national guidelines before forming a judgment about wind turbines near the Oude IJssel. As long as these are not available, the turbines will not be installed. Only afterward will we assess if the plans fit within the new guidelines.

112634.explanation: Traffic is becoming increasingly busy in Doetinchem. This will only get worse due to the closure of Keppel. The CDA has always supported the Sliekstraat route as it is the missing link in our accessibility and thus traffic safety.

112576.explanation: Quality and suitability should lead in forming a council, not gender. Forcibly equal numbers limit freedom of choice and can come at the expense of the best candidates. Of course, we strive for diversity, but you achieve that by stimulating talent, not by quotas.

112577.explanation: Residents should always be well and timely involved, but referenda delay decisions and often provide a simple yes/no choice on complex dossiers. Representatives are there precisely to weigh interests integrally and be accountable for them.

112580.explanation: Resident initiatives for sustainable energy deserve support, but not a blank check. The municipality can assist with startup and regulations, while residents primarily invest themselves. That way, we accelerate sustainability without letting costs spiral out of control.

112581.explanation: Doetinchem already makes a substantial contribution with the planned shelter. An additional asylum seekers' center (AZC) would increase pressure on the housing market, livability, and safety. Therefore, we do not support a new AZC location.

112592.explanation: Music education is valuable, but municipal funds should be used where they are needed most. The municipality can encourage schools and cultural institutions so that every child is exposed to music, without expensive generic subsidies per child.

112593.explanation: Sports clubs are important, but they are not an extension of the municipal treasury. If a club is structurally struggling, it calls for better organization or indicates something about supply and demand-not extra public money.

112596.explanation: Camera surveillance is a useful tool but not an end in itself. In Doetinchem, we deploy it targeted and temporarily in places where it truly contributes to safety and increasing the chance of catching offenders, not by simply installing more cameras everywhere.

112602.explanation: Vacancy caused by deferred maintenance is not solved by subsidies; that actually rewards poor ownership. First, fully utilize what is possible under the current zoning; only then do we discuss a different function.

112603.explanation: Social assistance is a springboard to work, not a final destination. Those receiving benefits contribute to society to the best of their ability; that is fair to those who work daily and helps people regain rhythm, structure, and opportunities for employment.

112604.explanation: Municipal charges are kept as low as possible for everyone. For those who absolutely cannot pay, exemption remains a necessary safety net, strictly tested for ability to pay and aimed at enabling people to carry their costs themselves again.

112610.explanation: An environmental zone does not suit Doetinchem. We do not opt for unrealistic zero-emission zones but for realistic steps toward cleaner mobility, allowing residents and entrepreneurs to continue accessing their homes or businesses by car.

112611.explanation: Care is about quality and availability, not the background of individual care workers. With the current tight labor market, it is simply not feasible to always match on religion or culture; that would unnecessarily endanger necessary care.

112612.explanation: Price is important in youth care, but never decisive. Quality, effectiveness, and continuity of care for vulnerable young people come first; only then do you compare providers on a sharp, efficient price.

112613.explanation: Public funds should support those who need it most, not free products for everyone. Those who truly cannot pay can be helped through targeted poverty and welfare schemes instead of generic free provisions.

112617.explanation: More industrial estates are needed to provide space for growing and new businesses, thereby strengthening our position as a working municipality. This is crucial for additional jobs, a strong local economy, and to keep Doetinchem attractive for entrepreneurs and talent.

112618.explanation: Everyone should have a fair chance at social rental housing, without automatic priority for one group. Status holders are humanely accommodated with extra (flexible) housing so waiting times for other home seekers do not increase further.

112619.explanation: Making the recycling center completely free sounds appealing but leads to higher costs for all residents and encourages more, also unsorted, waste. The polluter must pay, not the thrifty resident.

112620.explanation: There are plenty of other ways to generate sustainable energy, and large solar fields affect the landscape. But if a farmer chooses this themselves, it fits in, and farming continues, we see no reason for a ban.

112624.explanation: Agreed. Doetinchem already has a maximum system for coffee shops, and we want to maintain it. New establishments increase pressure on the environment and safety; therefore, expanding the number of coffee shops is undesirable.

112629.explanation: In and around the city center, there is already more than enough parking capacity; the problem is more with findability and routing than with the number of spaces. Additional asphalt is unnecessary; better use of existing facilities is.

112630.explanation: The center is already largely car-free, and new plans make the inner city even more car-restricted, with parking around the center and good walking routes to shops and restaurants. This keeps the center accessible, but safe, green, and appealing to stay in.

112631.explanation: Such a neighborhood is stigmatizing and does not solve the cause of nuisance. Better is strict enforcement, help where needed, and tailored solutions in existing neighborhoods so that areas remain safe and livable.

112632.explanation: Young people from Doetinchem should have a fair chance to live in their own municipality and not be forced to leave. Local priority for part of the social rental housing helps them on the overheated housing market.

112633.explanation: Along the Oude IJssel, houses and villages are close to the area; without clear national distance norms, it is not wise to allow large wind turbines there now. Clear rules and protection for residents first, then possible wind turbines.

112634.explanation: A western bypass not only helps to access Doetinchem but also provides a structural detour route if the Dorpsstraat in Laag-Keppel is (again) closed to through traffic. This improves traffic safety and livability in Laag-Keppel and traffic flow around the city.

112577.explanation: We consider good participation with stakeholders more important.

112581.explanation: We are bound by the distribution law.

112596.explanation: For high-risk areas, it could be an addition.

112617.explanation: There is a waiting list of companies wanting to settle or expand. More industrial estates are necessary to keep our municipality vital.

112634.explanation: Realize the Sliekstraat route with the province.

112576.explanation: GBD believes quality takes precedence over diversity.

112577.explanation: GBD also supports this form of participation.

112580.explanation: Initiatives from residents and neighborhoods should receive financial support where possible, according to GBD.

112581.explanation: Municipality of Doetinchem already takes responsibility with the temporary shelter at Terborgseweg. GBD believes this is more than sufficient for the coming years.

112592.explanation: This is already arranged in most schools through our cultural company Amphion.

112593.explanation: But under certain conditions. Think about making buildings more sustainable.

112596.explanation: Camera surveillance in the city center should be permanent, and for other locations temporary/mobile surveillance. This is to increase safety where needed.

112602.explanation: Additional housing is always welcome these days, but it must be considered whether it is desirable and possible at that location. For example, not in the middle of an industrial estate.

112603.explanation: Given the shortage of volunteers and growing loneliness in society, this is a great solution for everyone. After all, you also receive something from society.

112604.explanation: This is a nice way to help those who are already struggling a little.

112610.explanation: Everyone decides for themselves which vehicle they use.

112611.explanation: Given the limited availability and affordability, this is not possible or desirable.

112612.explanation: It concerns the best price and quality ratio.

112613.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

112617.explanation: The current space on business parks should be used more efficiently.

112618.explanation: No, this is unfair to all other home seekers.

112619.explanation: This way the polluter pays and general expenses are kept as low as possible.

112620.explanation: No solar panels on agricultural or nature land. This can simply be done on roofs.

112629.explanation: The number in general is sufficient, only the parking spaces near facilities must be within a short distance.

112630.explanation: Everyone determines their own means of transportation.

112631.explanation: GBD thinks it’s a good idea if sufficient supervision and care are provided for it.

112632.explanation: GBD wants to keep young people in the municipality and therefore give them priority for all homes suitable for young people.

112633.explanation: No large-scale wind projects on Doetinchem’s territory. With our small area, this is not possible in our municipality. We cannot assess the health risks.

112634.explanation: To increase traffic safety in the rest of the municipality.

112576.explanation: D66 finds diversity important, but primarily it concerns the qualities in the council.

112577.explanation: It is already possible to hold a referendum in Doetinchem. D66 supports that, but only on specific topics.

112580.explanation: D66 wants to promote sustainable energy generation in Doetinchem. If support from the municipality is needed for this, D66 is open to it.

112581.explanation: D66 thinks that the municipality of Doetinchem should take its fair share regarding the reception of asylum seekers.

112592.explanation: D66 considers cultural education important, which includes music lessons at school.

112593.explanation: D66 wants to support sports, but initially considers it the responsibility of the clubs themselves.

112596.explanation: Camera surveillance can be an effective tool, but D66 finds it important to consider proportionality, costs, and privacy.

112602.explanation: D66 wants to convert vacant offices into homes to help reduce the housing shortage.

112603.explanation: D66 wants everyone to participate actively. If working is not possible, D66 wants to explore with these residents what they can do.

112604.explanation: D66 wants to waive municipal taxes for residents who cannot afford to pay them.

112610.explanation: D66 wants fewer polluting vehicles in the center, but a low-emission zone is not the appropriate measure for this.

112611.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize the quality of care. If a care provider delivers that quality, then their conviction or beliefs are of lesser importance.

112612.explanation: D66 thinks quality should come first, but also wants the municipality to make strict agreements about price.

112613.explanation: D66 wants to prevent people from falling into poverty, so that everyone is able to buy necessary products themselves.

112617.explanation: D66 thinks it is important that there are good (work)spaces for entrepreneurs and employees.

112618.explanation: D66 believes that everyone with urgency should quickly find a home, which also applies to status holders in a reception center.

112619.explanation: D66 finds it fair that those who put out waste also pay for the processing of that waste.

112620.explanation: D66 supports sustainable energy but believes there are already many solar panels on agricultural land and wants a cautious approach going forward.

112624.explanation: D66 thinks it is good that there are two coffee shops in Doetinchem. No new coffee shops need to be added.

112629.explanation: D66 wants the center to be accessible for pedestrians and cyclists and cars to stay as much as possible on the outskirts of the city.

112630.explanation: D66 wants the center to be accessible for pedestrians and cyclists and cars to stay as much as possible on the outskirts of the city.

112631.explanation: D66 supports mixed neighborhoods where different people live together and coexist. Placing people outside of normal neighborhoods does not belong to that.

112632.explanation: D66 considers it important that our youth find housing and therefore wants them to have priority for social rental homes.

112633.explanation: D66 supports sustainable energy generation and wants to comply with regional agreements. For wind turbines, we see only one possible location in the municipality of Doetinchem. We want health distance standards for our residents to be taken into account.

112634.explanation: D66 wants cars to stay on the edges of the city and to address increasing traffic pressure, a ring road is needed on the west side of Doetinchem.

112576.explanation: We value diversity, but quality comes first for us.

112577.explanation: This way residents can actively think along and share their opinions about significant and important issues affecting Doetinchem.

112580.explanation: We support residents who want to generate sustainable energy together, with financial support from the municipality based on co-financing.

112581.explanation: If an asylum seekers center is established, it can only be under clear conditions, with sufficient support from the local community and attention to safety.

112592.explanation: We believe it is important that every child has the opportunity to take lessons in music and other forms of art and culture with subsidies.

112593.explanation: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality can provide financial support, provided the club is sustainable and has a clear future vision.

112596.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance can contribute to safety, while always respecting privacy and ensuring careful deployment.

112602.explanation: Converting vacant office buildings into homes can help increase available housing and requires a tailored approach per location.

112603.explanation: Those able to do so should provide a socially meaningful contribution in return for social assistance benefits.

112604.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes for residents with low incomes remains a matter of individual assessment.

112610.explanation: The city center should be preserved as much as possible from polluting vehicles. In the long term, this can also apply to adjacent areas.

112611.explanation: We consider accessible and reachable healthcare more important. Other aspects such as religion, culture, or beliefs are subordinate to this.

112612.explanation: When selecting organizations for youth care, quality must be an important pillar, not primarily price.

112613.explanation: We see the provision of free tampons and sanitary pads mainly as a task for social organizations and healthcare.

112617.explanation: More business parks can contribute to economic development, while maintaining a good balance between living and working.

112618.explanation: We adhere to the national targets for status holders and do not consider extra priority for social rental housing desirable.

112619.explanation: Waste disposal at the recycling center must remain cost-covering; therefore, we do not support making it free of charge.

112620.explanation: We prefer to install solar panels on building roofs and not on agricultural land.

112624.explanation: We consider the current number of two coffeeshops in Doetinchem to be desirable.

112629.explanation: We are looking at expanding existing parking lots directly adjacent to the center to increase the number of parking spaces.

112630.explanation: We do not find a completely car-free center desirable. A low-traffic center is preferred, whereby residents must still have good access to their homes.

112631.explanation: We believe more in dispersal and social control. Therefore, creating a separate neighborhood for people causing nuisance is not desirable.

112632.explanation: Young people from Doetinchem should be able to receive priority in the allocation of social rental housing.

112633.explanation: We see wind turbines as industrial objects and therefore find their placement on the Oude IJssel inappropriate.

112634.explanation: We focus on the Sliekstraattracé as the missing link for a ring road on the west side of Doetinchem.

112576.explanation: We consider the suitability of a person more important than fixed quotas.

112577.explanation: Resident participation is important, but decision-making lies with the democratically elected council.

112580.explanation: The municipality should encourage sustainability measures. One possible option is establishing a foundation for a solar park with financial participation. Other measures can make use of national subsidies.

112581.explanation: We value compassion and care. We want to find a balance between taking our responsibility and accepting what residents can handle. Every municipality, including Gelderland-Zuid, must bear responsibility for the numbers currently required by the distribution law.

112592.explanation: Music and cultural education are important parts of upbringing and development.

112593.explanation: Sport is important. Support takes place through subsidies and participation grants.

112596.explanation: Cameras increase residents’ sense of safety. Therefore, we want to allow them in the center, so everyone can safely walk the streets at any time.

112602.explanation: We support converting offices into homes, but not with municipal subsidies.

112603.explanation: We encourage this because it allows people to continue participating meaningfully in society.

112604.explanation: We find it important that people with low incomes can continue to participate.

112610.explanation: We consider a healthy living environment and an attractive center important, provided entrepreneurs are not disproportionately affected and can manage this technically and financially (currently not even possible due to network congestion).

112611.explanation: We find it important that people receive care that aligns with their religion, culture, or beliefs.

112612.explanation: Quality and continuity come first. Price alone should never be leading with vulnerable youth. It must still fit within the available resources we receive from the national government.

112613.explanation: We prefer to help people via existing poverty policies rather than giving everyone free tampons and sanitary pads.

112617.explanation: Employment is crucial, but with care for the landscape and living environment.

112618.explanation: Humane shelter is important, but housing seekers must also be treated fairly. We seek a good balance.

112619.explanation: It is already partially free. Paying per use encourages careful waste management and prevents abuse.

112620.explanation: Our preference is solar panels on roofs; agricultural land is valuable.

112624.explanation: We want a cautious drug policy with a focus on safety and prevention.

112629.explanation: We choose a car-reduced and attractive city center.

112630.explanation: Fits with livability, safety, and quality of stay, provided it remains well accessible.

112631.explanation: Not to sideline people, but to better combine care, support, and safety. It should be coordinated regionally so every municipality takes its responsibility.

112632.explanation: Housing allocation must remain fair and transparent, ensuring different groups are treated fairly and no group is structurally disadvantaged.

112633.explanation: Residents, nature, and landscape must be respected. Local participation should be central. Decisions will only be made once national standards are known.

112634.explanation: Only if necessity and added value are clear; not a given project.

112580.explanation: Ultimately, they will save on their energy costs themselves.

112593.explanation: Sports clubs must draw up a balanced budget.

112603.explanation: A reciprocal obligation may be asked.

112604.explanation: A sliding scale would be ideal.

112613.explanation: Free for those who really need it.

112617.explanation: Must never come at the expense of nature reserves or agricultural land.

112620.explanation: Agricultural land is not intended for that.

112624.explanation: Supply and demand. Some restrictions regarding location.

112629.explanation: No endless searching for a spot.

112631.explanation: That would also have an attracting effect.

112634.explanation: It will again come at the expense of agricultural land.

112580.explanation: There is already a subsidy for this.

112581.explanation: Stop asylum seekers, first take stock.

112592.explanation: As broad an educational offer as possible.

112593.explanation: It is important that everyone can play sports; this is a condition.

112602.explanation: More homes for the residents of our municipality.

112611.explanation: Together and for each other, regardless of faith, culture or belief.

112619.explanation: Waste is a responsibility for all of us.

112620.explanation: No solar parks in rural areas.

112624.explanation: Also meet the needs of a part of society. Otherwise, an illegal circuit may arise.

112629.explanation: Create those in the ring around the center.

112631.explanation: No “boxes” but also fill in “neighbourhood” with this.

112632.explanation: Look at your own residents with policy, including the youth.

112576.explanation: Volt believes that the city council should reflect society.

112577.explanation: Volt is not in favor of a referendum. Carefully introduced legislation cannot be replaced by a yes/no question. Citizens' assemblies are processes in which citizen participation takes place on various topics over longer periods.

112580.explanation: Volt supports local initiatives to generate sustainable energy.

112581.explanation: The distribution law is an effective way to fairly distribute migrant influx across our country. Doetinchem must also take its responsibility in this.

112592.explanation: Volt believes every child should have the opportunity to take music lessons at the music school.

112593.explanation: Each sports club is responsible for sound financial management. The municipality can advise, facilitate and support.

112596.explanation: Volt is not in favor of large-scale camera surveillance. We are open to strategic, transparent, location-based camera surveillance at locations with real safety issues. Social control, behavioral change and better design of public spaces are at least as effective as cameras.

112602.explanation: Housing is a right for everyone. By converting existing offices, new homes can be realized quickly. Subsidies for this reduce the profit difference with new construction. The municipality, as subsidy provider, maintains control and can set conditions on rental prices and allocation criteria.

112603.explanation: Instead of a mandatory counter-performance, Volt chooses support towards work with language and training programs. We recognize voluntary effort alongside the obligation to apply for jobs. We experiment with, among other things, basic income to give people more space and control over their efforts.

112604.explanation: Volt advocates more financial security and inclusivity through municipal schemes. Debt forgiveness is part of this. We believe in experimenting with measures such as debt forgiveness, basic income and other arrangements to protect the vulnerable.

112610.explanation: Volt is positive about introducing an environmental zone as a means to make the center car-reduced, cleaner and healthier. What works in big cities can also work in Doetinchem: exclude polluting vehicles combined with smart logistics such as a transport hub and adjusted delivery times.

112611.explanation: Volt considers care a basic right: accessible to everyone. Freedom of choice is a central principle. People must be able to trust their caregiver. At the same time, there is pressure in healthcare. We do not have the means to offer each resident a care provider of their choice.

112612.explanation: Volt believes that the quality of care should be the focus. Price should be taken into account but must not be leading.

112613.explanation: Volt wants to introduce free menstrual products in the municipality of Doetinchem to combat poverty. Menstrual products should also be free in other public spaces for those who need them.

112617.explanation: Volt wants to optimally use existing space on business parks but companies that innovate, digitize and become more sustainable also deserve a place in Doetinchem. This does require a critical look at the type of company. Providing extra employment is a decisive factor.

112618.explanation: Refugees with a permit occupy valuable places in asylum centers and do not have the chance to build a long waiting time. All people are equal. Therefore, everyone must be able to get priority in specific situations.

112619.explanation: This fits with the ambition of Doetinchem to be 100% circular by 2050. Being able to deliver for free also prevents dumping in nature.

112620.explanation: Volt wants to accelerate the energy transition by allowing solar panels on agricultural land with zero ecological value. Only under strict conditions, such as requiring the EcoCertified Solar Parks label, so that solar parks strengthen nature and soil.

112624.explanation: Volt believes the number of coffeeshops should be proportional to the number of residents. At about 60,000 residents, two coffeeshops are appropriate. With future population growth, establishing a third coffeeshop can be considered, provided it is done carefully and does not cause extra nuisance.

112629.explanation: Volt believes it is not about more parking spaces, but better use of existing space. Smart distinctions between short and long-term parking and clear rules keep the center accessible without burdening extra space.

112630.explanation: With exceptions for residents and destination traffic, the center should be car-free as quickly as possible according to Volt.

112631.explanation: There are proven solutions such as Skaeve Huse: small-scale housing with guidance aimed at reducing nuisance and offering perspective. Success requires a widely supported approach; a citizens' assembly can help carefully develop this together with local residents.

112632.explanation: We believe the current allocation policy is already sufficient.

112633.explanation: Volt is for sustainable energy but wants clarity about location and impact on the environment when making decisions.

112634.explanation: A western bypass reduces traffic through the center, improves flow and increases safety. At the same time, we want to keep investing in other, more sustainable forms of transport.

statements.title: A bypass must also be built on the west side of Doetinchem.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in Doetinchem.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Doetinchem must introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: A separate small neighborhood should be created with housing for people who cause nuisance in regular homes or on the street.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The residents of Doetinchem must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Doetinchem moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: Young people from Doetinchem must be given priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Jongeren uit Doetinchem moeten voorrang krijgen op een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
Doetinchem must create more business parks.
The center of Doetinchem must become car-free.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: There must be wind turbines along the Oude IJssel.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
